20.overview: While at an old record store, Peter and the guys recount tales of three rock legends: Jim Morrison, Muddy Waters and Elton John.

20.name: Must Love Dogs
20.file: /srv/media/Series/Family.Guy/Family.Guy.S20/Family Guy S20E03 - Doit aimer les chiens (Must Love Dogs).mkv
20.overview: Quagmire meets an avid dog lover, and pretends Brian is his dog in an attempt to win her over. Meanwhile, Chris helps Stewie track down the culprit who stole his Halloween candy.

20.name: 80's Guy
20.file: /srv/media/Series/Family.Guy/Family.Guy.S20/Family Guy S20E04 - Coincé dans les années 80 (80's Guy).mkv
20.overview: Impelled by nostalgia, Peter recreates movie scenes from his favorite decade; Stewie and Doug compete to retrieve a Frisbee stuck at the top of the jungle gym.

20.name: Brief Encounter
20.overview: When Peter and Quagmire accidentally switch underwear, they begin to mimic the other's behavior. Stewie and Doug agree to commit each other's murders.

20.name: Cootie & The Blowhard
20.file: /srv/media/Series/Family.Guy/Family.Guy.S20/Family Guy S20E06 - Les bibittes mortelles (Cootie & The Blowhard).mkv
20.overview: Stewie takes drastic measures when he thinks he is diagnosed with terminal cooties. Peter becomes infatuated with Bonnie's cooking.

20.name: Peterschmidt Manor
20.file: /srv/media/Series/Family.Guy/Family.Guy.S20/Family Guy S20E07 - Le manoir Peterschmidt (Peterschmidt Manor).mkv
20.overview: Lois and her father attend the funeral of Lois' childhood nanny. Peter and the rest of the family turn Pewterschmidt mansion into a hotel.

20.name: The Birthday Bootlegger
20.file: /srv/media/Series/Family.Guy/Family.Guy.S20/Family Guy S20E08 - Anniversaire de contrebande (The Birthday Bootlegger).mkv
20.overview: A new boss quells Peter’s favorite part about work: office birthdays. Stewie is put in detention and comes out a ruffian.

20.name: The Fatman Always Rings Twice
20.file: /srv/media/Series/Family.Guy/Family.Guy.S20/Family Guy S20E09 - Le gros tas sonne toujours deux fois (The Fatman Always Rings Twice).mkv
20.overview: In noir-style, Peter/Mac investigates the disappearance of Meg/Sister Megan.

20.name: Christmas Crime
20.file: /srv/media/Series/Family.Guy/Family.Guy.S20/Family Guy S20E10 - Le criminel de Noël (Christmas Crime).mkv
20.overview: After a Christmas tree lightning ceremony Brian crashes into Mayor West's nativity scene causing outrage.

20.name: Mister Act
20.file: /srv/media/Series/Family.Guy/Family.Guy.S20/Family Guy S20E11 - Frères sans coeur (Mister Act).mkv
20.overview: An accident leaves Peter with a high-pitched voice, so Jesus coaches him into fending off the choir boys; Stewie is attracted to Lois' new masculine physique.

20.name: The Lois Quagmire
20.file: /srv/media/Series/Family.Guy/Family.Guy.S20/Family Guy S20E12 - Lois Quagmire (The Lois Quagmire).mkv
20.overview: At odds with Peter, Lois takes Quagmire to her high school reunion; the rest of the family must cover up the death of a pizza delivery man.

20.name: Lawyer Guy
20.file: /srv/media/Series/Family.Guy/Family.Guy.S20/Family Guy S20E13 - Le Nouvel Avocat (Lawyer Guy).mkv
20.overview: Peter hires the town's new lawyer, Brick Baker, to represent him in litigation against his new neighbor -- who turns out to be Brick; Brian and Stewie become lobstermen and save Rupert from the perils of the deep.

20.name: HBO-No
20.overview: The Griffins tell their own versions of three HBO shows: "Game of Thrones," "Succession" and "Big Little Lies."

20.name: Hard Boiled Meg
20.file: /srv/media/Series/Family.Guy/Family.Guy.S20/Family Guy S20E15 - Meg, la dur à cuire (Hard Boiled Meg).mkv
20.overview: Meg is invigorated by her new role as a getaway driver. Meanwhile, Quagmire can't rid himself of the hiccups and implores Peter to kill him.

20.name: Prescription Heroine
20.file: /srv/media/Series/Family.Guy/Family.Guy.S20/Family Guy S20E16 - Héroïne sur ordonnance (Prescription Heroine).mkv
20.overview: After Brian sustains an injury, Lois gets hooked on his painkillers. Meanwhile, Cleveland refurbishes Peter's ping pong table, which becomes the hot spot in the neighborhood.

20.name: All About Alana
20.file: /srv/media/Series/Family.Guy/Family.Guy.S20/Family Guy S20E17 - Alana la nounou (All About Alana).mkv
20.overview: When Lois' piano student, Alana, comes to work for the Griffins, Lois is initially glad for the help, until she realizes Alana's intentions are more sinister than she thought.

20.name: Girlfriend, Eh?
20.file: /srv/media/Series/Family.Guy/Family.Guy.S20/Family Guy S20E18 - La Blonde de Chris (Girlfriend, Eh).mkv
20.overview: Peter and Chris take a road trip to Canada to prove the existence of Chris' girlfriend. Meanwhile, Lois and Meg compete for the attention of a handsome handyman.

20.name: First Blood
20.overview: The guys' physical prowess is tested at Mayor West's dude ranch. Meanwhile, Stewie thinks he's gotten his first period.

20.name: The Jersey Bore
20.overview: Peter attends a business conference in Atlantic City with Preston. Meanwhile, Stewie helps Chris prepare for his role in Adam West High's rendition of "Romeo and Juliet".

21.name: Oscars Guy
21.overview: "Family Guy" retells three Oscar-winning stories: "Silence of the Lambs," "American Beauty" and "Forrest Gump".

21.name: Bend or Blockbuster
21.overview: Resolved to enjoy a family movie night, the Griffins take a road trip to Bend, Oregon, to rent a VHS from the last Blockbuster in existence.

21.name: A Wife-Changing Experience
21.overview: Joe accidentally sees Lois naked, leading to a reinvigoration of his sex life with Bonnie. Meanwhile, Stewie is gifted an electric SUV and quickly embodies the "Range Rover" lifestyle.

21.name: The Munchurian Candidate
21.overview: Lois hypnotizes Peter into satisfying her sexual proclivities. Meanwhile, Stewie renovates his treehouse in an attempt to win over Brian and Chris.

21.name: Unzipped Code
21.overview: Cleveland is fired as a mailman after an ignominious mishap, but flourishes in his new job at the brewery.

21.name: Happy Holo-ween
21.overview: Peter makes a hologram of himself in order to get out of menial household duties.

21.name: The Stewaway
21.overview: During a game of "hide and seek," Stewie takes refuge in Quagmire's suitcase, only to wind up in Paris. Meanwhile, Peter and Lois take a getaway to Vermont, where Lois is intent on breaking Bonnie's Instagram "like" record.

21.name: Get Stewie
21.overview: Stewie finds himself in public disgrace after a popstar unleashes her fan base on him for a benign comment. Meanwhile, Peter gets lap band surgery and enjoys the merriment of excess skin.

21.name: Carny Knowledge
21.overview: Peter and Chris finish last in a "Generation Gap" game at a carnival, forcing them to try to reconnect. Brian starts to date a carny, whom he can't break up with even after witnessing some of her crude habits.

21.name: The Candidate
21.overview: Stewie and Doug compete for the honor of class “snack captain”.

21.name: Love Story Guy
21.overview: The guys recount their first true love stories, leading to parodies of "Castaway," "Dirty Dancing" and various Meg Ryan rom-coms.

21.name: Old West
21.overview: The guys try to help Mayor Wild West reconnect with his estranged father, Old West.

21.name: Single White Dad
21.overview: Peter is mistaken for being a single parent, and relishes in the perks that come with it.

21.name: White Meg Can't Jump
21.overview: Meg joins the Adam West High basketball team, and her play is fueled by Peter's lambasting from the crowd. Meanwhile, Stewie goes down a rabbit hole of conspiracies, which ultimately ends with him developing multiple personalities.

21.name: Adoptation
21.overview: To restore his public image, Carter adopts a young girl from an orphanage, drawing jealousy from Lois.

21.name: The Bird Reich
21.overview: To take advantage of a work policy granting leave in the event of a pet's death, Peter adopts the oldest bird he can find... and realizes it used to belong to Hitler. Meanwhile, after giving Stewie a signed copy of his book, Brian finds it in a free library, causing Brian to give up writing.

21.name: A Bottle Episode
21.overview: Lois is blocked from a vacation rental app and accused of stealing after the family's trip to Nantucket. Meanwhile, Stewie and Brian buy saltwater taffy in bulk, and try their hands at door-to-door sales in the community.

21.name: Vat Man & Rob 'Em
21.overview: When Peter gets a job as the new night watchman, he and the guys turn Pawtucket Brewery into an after-hours bar. Meanwhile, Stewie and Brian's plan to collect "lost dog" return money backfires when Brian is used for a dangerous TikTok stunt.

21.name: From Russia With Love
21.overview: After Brian's Instagram is hacked, Meg takes Stewie and Brian to Russia, only to realize she'd rather stay there. Meanwhile, Lois reverts to her tormented teenage self when Quagmire starts dating her high school bully.

21.name: Adult Education
21.overview: Meg gets married in Russia but returns home after discovering she was used for a green card marriage. Meanwhile, Chris, in need of an extracurricular activity, joins Principal Shepherd in shooting pornos at the high school.

22.name: Fertilized Megg
22.overview: Meg agrees to be Bruce and Jeffery's surrogate causing a pregnant Meg to annoy her family. But, when the couple fails to pick up the baby, the Griffins must take care of their new family.

22.name: Supermarket Pete
22.overview: With the brewery under construction, Lois implores Peter to take a new job, but grows irate when he begins work at the grocery store. Meanwhile, armed with his police stories, Joe and Brian team up to write their own true crime novel.

22.name: A Stache from the Past
22.overview: When Peter loses the Griffins' home in a reverse mortgage, he and Mayor West travel to confront the man advertising the scheme, Tom Selleck. Meanwhile, Stewie and Brian open a booth at the flea market, leading to a rivalry with Bruce.

22.name: Old World Harm
22.overview: Peter and Lois vacation in Florida, only to discover they've rented a condo in a retirement community. Back at home, Brian helps Stewie take his first shower.

22.name: Baby, It's Cold Inside
22.overview: Peter is emasculated when, on their anniversary, he abandons Lois amidst a shark attack. Meanwhile, Stewie finds a mini fridge and lives the dorm lifestyle.

22.name: Boston Stewie
22.overview: Stewie tries to civilize his half-brother, Boston Stewie, so that he may be adopted. Meanwhile, Meg and Chris start a venture in which people online pay Chris to sit on and smash cupcakes.

22.name: Snap(ple) Decision
22.overview: Winner of a Snapple contest, Lois uses her winnings to take Meg on a tropical vacation. Flaunting their wealth, the two are kidnapped. Meanwhile, Stewie and Brian undergo "friendship therapy."

22.name: Baking Sad
22.overview: Meg discovers her tears produce delicious cookies, and her, along with her siblings, enjoy a profit until the success makes her too happy to cry. Meanwhile, Peter and the guys launch a "male talk show."

22.name: The Return of the King (of Queens)
22.overview: Lois decides to sell the family's television to help pay for Christmas presents, but when Kevin James gets upset over losing Peter as a viewer, he apparates into the Griffin house.

22.name: Cabin Pressure
22.overview: When the Griffins win a trip to Maine at Peter's company picnic, they decide to invite their friends on the vacation. Tensions run high and things soon spiral out of control when the cabin burns down.

22.name: Teacher's Heavy Pet
22.overview: Lois joins Chris's school as a substitute teacher, launching him into the cool kids club... but only time will tell how long he remains a member.

22.name: Take This Job and Love It
22.overview: Peter and his friends wonder aloud about what their dream jobs would be.

22.name: Lifeguard Meg
22.overview: Stewie and Brian become overwhelmed after winning a coffee shop. Peter and his friends annoy Meg at her new lifeguard job when they don't follow the pool rules.

22.name: Fat Actor
22.overview: Peter tries to protest Hollywood after learning that Brad Pitt was cast in a biopic about a politician. After Pitt injures himself on set, Peter must act out the role himself.

22.name: Faith No More
22.overview: Brian becomes romantically interested in someone and it inspires him to use Stewie's time machine. Strange consequences result from their journey.

3.name: The Thin White Line (1)
3.overview: Brian's keen sense of smell gets him a job with the police department.

3.name: Brian Does Hollywood (2)
3.overview: Brian's first job in Hollywood is directing a pornographic film.

3.name: Mr. Griffin Goes to Washington
3.overview: The tobacco industry sends Peter to Washington to present a speech about smoking.

3.name: One If by Clam, Two If by Sea
3.overview: When a hurricane strikes Quohog, everything is destroyed including The Drunken Clam, which is bought out by a Brit who turns it into an English pub. As it happens, pub owner Nigel Pinchley and his family move in next door to the Griffins, and Stewie tries to teach Nigel's Cockney-accented 3-year-old daughter how to speak proper English.

3.name: And the Wiener Is...
3.overview: Peter joins a gun club to compensate for his lack of physical assets; Meg becomes the target of the "cool" gang's wrath.

3.name: Death Lives
3.overview: After a near-death experience on his anniversary, Peter learns the importance of spending time with Lois.

3.name: Lethal Weapons
3.overview: New Yorkers invade the town in the fall; Lois hones her martial-arts skills.

3.name: The Kiss Seen Around the World
3.overview: Meg lands an internship working with the school nerd at a television station.

3.name: Mr. Saturday Knight
3.overview: Peter decides to pursue his dream career as a knight.

3.name: Fish Out of Water
3.overview: When Peter decides to become a fisherman, he takes out a large loan to buy a fishing boat and unwittingly gives up everything he owns as collateral.

3.name: Emission Impossible
3.overview: Lois' pregnant sister inspires her and Peter to have another baby.

3.name: To Love and Die in Dixie
3.overview: The Griffins are put into the witness protection program.

3.name: Screwed the Pooch
3.overview: Peter and Lois go to the Pewterschmidts for the weekend, bringing Brian along. Brian, meanwhile, has some "issues" of his own to work out. Find out if Brian's future is to make puppies or become "less" than he is.

3.name: Peter Griffin: Husband, Father... Brother?
3.overview: While trying to teach Chris about his Irish roots, Peter stumbles upon Nate Griffin, a black man in his family tree.  Peter tries to embrace his new-found heritage, but can’t quite get the hang of it.  When Lois’ father, Carter Pewtershmidt, sits the Griffin kids down for a look at his family genealogy, a dark secret is uncovered.

3.name: Ready, Willing, and Disabled
3.overview: Joe gets discouraged, losing his self-confidence, after he loses a thief in a chase.  Peter wants to help Joe get back to his old self so he volunteers to help train him for the decathlon at the Olympics for handicapped athletes. Peter puts steroids into Joe's drink, pushing Joe on to victory.  Meanwhile, Stewie, Meg, and Brian fight over $26.

3.name: A Very Special Family Guy Freakin' Christmas
3.overview: Christmas comes to Quahog!  While Lois attempt to make the holidays perfect for the Griffin family, Peter messes it up.  Meanwhile, Stewie learns of Santa Claus’ ability to keep 24-hour surveillance on all the world’s children.

3.name: Brian Wallows and Peter's Swallows
3.overview: Brian is sentenced to care for a crabby old woman; Peter's beard becomes a home for birds.

3.name: From Method to Madness
3.overview: Stewie attempts a career in show business; Meg dates a nudist.

3.name: Stuck Together, Torn Apart
3.overview: Peter's jealously forces a trial separation; Stewie and Brian get glued together.

3.name: Road to Europe
3.overview: Stewie goes to England to take refuge in his favorite television show, while Lois embarrasses Peter at a KISS concert.

3.name: Family Guy Viewer Mail #1
3.overview: Viewer mail inspires three stories in which Peter loses his bones, the Griffins gain superpowers, and Peter and his friends appear as eight-year-old "Little Rascals."

3.name: When You Wish Upon a Weinstein
3.overview: The day that Peter buys volcano insurance is the day that Lois becomes fed up with their constant money problems.

4.name: North by North Quahog
4.overview: Peter and Lois decide to go on a second honeymoon. But when Peter crashes the car, he decides to pose as Mel Gibson to stay in his fancy hotel suite, where they find a copy of a "The Passion of the Christ" sequel. They attempt to destroy the film and end up being chased by two priests who went to retrieve the film.

4.name: Fast Times at Buddy Cianci Jr. High
4.overview: Brian becomes a substitute teacher at Chris' school after his teacher wins the lottery. When Brian is moved to teach a class of troubled kids, Chris falls in love with his new teacher, Mrs. Lockhart, who promises to love Chris only if he kills her husband. When Lois finds out, she and Stewie decide to teach Chris a lesson.

4.name: Blind Ambition
4.overview: After Mort bowls a perfect game, and Quagmire gets a key to the city by performing CPR, Peter notices he has nothing to be proud of. He then attempts to achieve fame by setting the world's record for most nickels swallowed. However, a slight miscalculation leaves him blind with nickel poisoning.

4.name: Don't Make Me Over
4.overview: When Meg gets turned down for a date, she asks her family for support. Lois ends up taking her to the mall, where she gets the ultimate makeover. Meanwhile, Peter tries to start a band with the guys, but when their first gig fails, the family ends up performing. Impressed by Meg's singing, a record producer signs the family, and their travels eventually land them on "Saturday Night Live."

4.name: The Cleveland–Loretta Quagmire
4.overview: When Peter hears Loretta scream, he tries to help out, but discovers Loretta is cheating on Cleveland. Peter figures out that it was Quagmire and decides to tell Cleveland about it. But when Loretta walks out on Cleveland he doesn't take it lightly.

4.name: Petarded
4.overview: After winning a game of Trivial Pursuit, Peter is convinced he is a genius.  To prove Peter wrong, Brian suggests he take a test of the MacArthur Genius Grant.  The test results say that Peter is technically mentally challenged.  Peter has no problem abusing this handicap until Lois is injured and the kids are taken away because Peter is not mentally fit for parenting.

4.name: Brian the Bachelor
4.overview: "The Bachelorette" comes to Quahog and Brian is chosen to be a contestant.  Brian does not like the show, but participates just to have a vacation only to wind up falling in love with the bachelorette.  Meanwhile, Chris gets a nasty pimple on his cheek, which talks to Chris and likes to cause mischief.

4.name: 8 Simple Rules for Buying My Teenage Daughter
4.overview: Peter runs up his tab at Mort’s Pharmacy.  When he doesn’t have the money to pay, he decides to sell Meg to the Goldmans.  Neil is dating another girl which makes Meg jealous, and Stewie falls in love with Liddane, his new babysitter.

4.name: Breaking Out Is Hard to Do
4.overview: Lois’ kleptomania lands her in jail, leaving the Griffin household in a worse state of disarray than usual.  Peter and the family break her out and end up fugitives in Asian Town.

4.name: Model Misbehavior
4.overview: Lois fulfills a lifelong dream of being a model.  Stewie starts on a multi-level marketing company.  Brian in need of cash to buy his de-worming medication agrees to become his salesman.

4.name: Peter's Got Woods
4.overview: Peter and Brian’s friendship becomes strained after Brian begins dating one of Meg’s high school teachers.  Peter finds a replacement friend in James Woods, who is visiting Quahog because of a suggestion to rename James Woods High after Dr. Martin Luther King Jr.

4.name: Perfect Castaway
4.overview: When Peter and the guys decide to go fishing, they end up caught in a storm. Stranded on a desert island, their families fear the worst. Rescued by a cruise ship months later, Peter surprises Lois at home, where he finds out that she has a new husband.

4.name: Jungle Love
4.overview: When Chris is supposed to be hazed for a school tradition, he runs off to South America; Peter quickly becomes frustrated by his new job at the beer factory.

4.name: PTV
4.overview: After a “wardrobe malfunction” occurs during a live broadcast, the FCC begins to censor all of Peter’s favorite shows.  As a result, Peter creates his own station, PTV, which broadcasts from the Griffin home.  Peter and Brian create their own edgy programming until the FCC shows up to shut down PTV and censor all controversial material in Quahog.

4.name: Brian Goes Back to College
4.overview: After writing a story for a local magazine, Brian gets a job at The New Yorker.

4.name: The Courtship of Stewie's Father
4.overview: Lois thinks Stewie is trying to kill her due to his lack of quality time with his father, so she orders Peter to spend more time with Stewie. Meanwhile Chris starts a friendship with Herbert.

4.name: The Fat Guy Strangler
4.overview: Lois discovers she has a brother named Patrick and takes the family cross-country to meet him.  It turns out; he’s locked up in a mental institution.  Lois thinks he’s sane, so she checks him out to live with the Griffins.  Meanwhile, Peter starts the Society for the Acceptance of Fat Men.  But when all the fat men of Quahog are strangled one-by-one the group lives in fear.  Who could the “Fat Man Strangler” be?

4.name: The Father, the Son, and the Holy Fonz
4.overview: When Peter’s dad comes to visit, he tries to impose his religious beliefs on the family.  But after a mix-up with the holy-water leaves Stewie in a bubble, Peter decides to form a church worshiping his hero, the Fonz.

4.name: Brian Sings and Swings
4.overview: After Brian has a near-death experience in the hospital, he decides to lead a new life. He ends up getting a nightly singing gig with Frank Sinatra Jr., but his new lifestyle doesn't last very long. Meanwhile, Meg, after joining a new after-school group, pretends to be a lesbian.

4.name: Patriot Games
4.overview: At his high school reunion, Peter tries to impress his fellow classmates. When Peter, heading for the bathroom, charges through the crowd of people, impresses quarterback Tom Brady, who offers Peter a spot on the New England Patriots. Meanwhile, Brian owes Stewie money, after Stewie becomes a sports bookie.

4.name: I Take Thee Quagmire
4.overview: When Peter wins a maid for a week as a prize on a game show, Quagmire gains an interest in her, and they eventually marry. Quagmire, stricken with his bachelor ways, tries to get out of the marriage. Meanwhile, Lois stops breast-feeding Stewie, who, as a result, goes through withdrawals.

4.name: Sibling Rivalry
4.overview: Peter gets a vasectomy and loses all his sex drive. Lois begins to gain weight, and as a result, Peter's regains his interest in his wife. When Lois' habits get out of control, she has a heart attack. The surgeon must remove Lois' fat to reach her heart, returning her to her normal weight. Meanwhile, Stewie's half-brother Bertram returns to confront him, where they have a war for control over the playground.

4.name: Deep Throats
4.overview: Meg becomes an intern for Mayor West. Brian, who is sure that the mayor is corrupt, tries to out him. Brian and Stewie, while undercover, discover that Meg is more than just an intern. Meanwhile, Peter and Lois go back to their folk singing days at a local talent show. When they begin using drugs, Chris stages an intervention.

4.name: Peterotica
4.overview: After reading an erotic novel, Peter gets the idea to write one himself. Carter gets sued for publishing Peter's book on tape and loses all his money, where he is forced to become a middle-class citizen. Meanwhile, Stewie tries to train for the Olympics.

4.name: You May Now Kiss the... Uh... Guy Who Receives
4.overview: Brian's gay cousin Jasper visits the Griffin house and announces that he is engaged to his partner and wants to be married at their house.  When Mayor West bans gay marriage in an effort to cover up and bring attention away from a scandal of his own, Brian resorts to hostile actions to try to change the Mayor's mind.  Lois and Peter question their view towards gay marriage while Chris joins the "Young Republicans" Club to impress a girl.

4.name: Petergeist
4.overview: Peter wants to top Joe, who just built a new home theater system, so he starts to build a multiplex in his backyard.  In doing so, he discovers an Indian burial ground.  He finds an Indian chief's skull and the Griffin house becomes haunted.  Stewie gets sucked into the television and strange events follow as they try to get Stewie back from the poltergeist.

4.name: The Griffin Family History
4.overview: When burglars break into the house, the Griffins take cover in the attic where they discover Peter has built a 'panic room'. They go in it, and Peter tells them about his history. They send Meg for food but the burglars catch her. The rest of the Griffins stay behind and Peter accidentally floods the room and they're going to drown. Luckily Joe gets them out and we find out Meg has been accused of sexual harassment by the burglars.

4.name: Stewie B. Goode
4.overview: After losing at a swim competition, Stewie trys to eliminate his rival by blowing up a lifeguard tower. His plan backfires though, and Stewie has a near death experience that seems to change him.

4.name: Bango Was His Name Oh!
4.overview: Stewie convinces Brian to go on a road trip with him to San Francisco to search for a man Stewie believes is his real father.

4.name: Stu & Stewie's Excellent Adventure
4.overview: Stewie finds out that his life in the future is miserable. So he decides to go back in time and change events for the better.

5.name: Stewie Loves Lois
5.overview: Stewie becomes obsessed with Lois, after she saves Rupert. Though Lois doesn't take to the new Stewie all too well. Meanwhile, Peter thinks his doctor is molesting him, when he undergoes a prostate exam.

5.name: Mother Tucker
5.overview: Peter's mom gets a divorce. Peter feels abandoned until he sees a father figure in his mom's new boyfriend, Tom Tucker. Meanwhile, Brian gets his own NPR style show on the radio.

5.name: Hell Comes to Quahog
5.overview: After Peter uses Meg's money to buy himself a tank, she gets a job at the new Superstore in town. However, the Superstore puts all the shops in town out of business and sucks up the city's power supply. Brian and Stewie team up to save the town from the corporate giant.

5.name: Saving Private Brian
5.overview: Chris is approached to join the military. Brian goes to the recruiting headquarters to voice his opinion, when Stewie signs himself and Brian up for the military. They pass Boot Camp and are shipped to Iraq, where they try to get honorably discharged by doing anything they can. Meanwhile, Chris decides to join a goth band.

5.name: Whistle While Your Wife Works
5.overview: When Peter gets hurt and can't work, his boss tells him he needs to speed things up. So Lois goes to the brewery to help him out. Meanwhile, Stewie finds out that Brian is dating a very attractive but not very intelligent woman. He tries to get Brian to break up with her, but Brian can't do it.

5.name: Prick Up Your Ears
5.overview: Lois discovers that Chris has some bad information about sex and she volunteers to teach a sex-ed class at the high school. Some parents become upset with her brutal honesty, and they have her replaced. A conservative christian comes to Quahog and gets rid of the condoms at James Woods High. As a result the kids find an interesting loophole. Meanwhile Stewie, terrified of the Tooth Fairy, decides to set traps to destroy him.

5.name: Chick Cancer
5.overview: Stewie ties the knot with his old flame Olivia, but their relationship quickly turns, and becomes normal dull marriage. It all escalates when they go on a double date with Brian and Jillian. Meanwhile, Lois introduces Peter to chick flicks, so he decides to make his own movie with a plot based on every chick movie ever.

5.name: Barely Legal
5.overview: Meg can't find a date to her junior prom, and even though he is dating Jillian, Brian ends up going with Meg. At the prom, Brian gets drunk to make the night pass, but ends up making out with Meg. Meg begins to think of Brian as her boyfriend and becomes obsessed with spending every moment with him. Meanwhile, Peter, Cleveland and Quagmire join the police force to help Brian.

5.name: Road to Rupert
5.overview: Brian sells Rupert at a garage sale. Stewie, who is upset with Brian, takes him cross country to Colorado to find his beloved teddy, and ends up having a ski-off to win back Rupert. Meanwhile, Peter loses his license, so Meg has to drive him around.

5.name: Peter's Two Dads
5.overview: After Peters dad, Francis, dies, Thelma tells him that his real father actually lives in Ireland. So Peter and Brian decide to head there in search of his father, who ends up being the town drunk. They decide to play a drinking game to prove his paternity. Meanwhile, Stewie acts out and Lois spanks him. Realizing that he likes being spanked, Stewie does whatever he can to get into trouble.

5.name: The Tan Aquatic with Steve Zissou
5.overview: When a bully harasses Chris, Peter goes to his defense, but ends up beating him up. Peter realizes bullying is fun and begins to bully everyone in the house. Finally, Chris stands up to Peter and beats him senseless. Meanwhile, Stewie goes out without sunscreen and becomes obsessed with getting a tan. After Brian leaves him in the tanning booth for six hours, Stewie gets sunburned and fears he has cancer. While waiting for the results of his cancer screening, Stewie begins to live his life as if he had only days to live and drives Brian crazy with his demands.

5.name: Airport '07
5.overview: Quagmire takes Peter to work but Peter messes things up, causing Quagmire to lose his job as a pilot. After not being able find another job, Peter and Cleveland make plans to help him get his old job back. When the plan fails, Quagmire receives advice from his idol, Hugh Hefner.

5.name: Bill & Peter's Bogus Journey
5.overview: Peter helps Bill Clinton with his flat tire, and they soon become friends. They begin spending a lot of time together and Lois begins to believe that Bill inflicting bad influence on Peter. But when she confronts Bill, they end up having sex. Peter, upset that his wife would do this, tries to get back at Lois by having an affair of his own, but soon realizes that he loves her too much to do so. Meanwhile, Lois and Stewie try to potty-train Brian.

5.name: No Meals on Wheels
5.overview: The Griffin family decides to start their own restaurant, though have a hard time attracting customers. After Joe promises to help, Peter is excited at the fact of it becoming a local hangout for the police. However, things don't really turn out as hoped, and instead it begins to be popular with the handicapped. When Peter attempts to change the place, a sudden twist of irony quickly changes his mind.

5.name: Boys Do Cry
5.overview: Lois gets a job as the church organist, so the family decides to begin going back to church. When Stewie gets sick after receiving communion, the congregation becomes convinced that he has been possessed by the devil. While on the run, the family ends up in Texas, where Peter begins to fit right in with the cowboys, Stewie enters the "Little Miss Texas" pageant, and Chris and Meg sneak into George W. Bush's house.

5.name: No Chris Left Behind
5.overview: After Chris is expelled from school, he is sent to a boarding school that his grandfather Carter attended, but Chris doesn't exactly fit in. Meanwhile, the chicken returns to pick a fight with Peter.

5.name: It Takes a Village Idiot, and I Married One
5.overview: Lois runs for mayor of Quahog, though she doesn't become the front-runner until she dumbs down her political views. Once in office, she becomes corrupted with the power that she now holds, and begins accepting bribes so that she can buy herself expensive things. Meanwhile, Peter comes to enjoy being the "first lady" of Quahog, and all of the perks that go along with it.

5.name: Meet the Quagmires
5.overview: Death grants Peter's wish to go back in time to 1984 when he was 18, but only for one night. He ends up bailing on a date with Lois to hang out with a movie actress. When he returns to the present, he soon realizes that everything is anything but normal, as he is now married to the former actress, and Lois is with Quagmire. Meanwhile, Stewie, Chris and Meg have also taken on Quagmire's charming characteristics.

6.name: Blue Harvest
6.overview: Peter retells the story of Star Wars in this hour-long episode of Family Guy. When Princess Leia is captured by the evil Darth Vader the droids C-3PO and R2-D2 escape to the planet Tatooine to find help from the Rebel Alliance. There they encounter Luke Skywalker, Han Solo, Chewbacca and Obi-Wan Kenobi who infiltrate the Death Star then rescue the captive princess from the Death Star and return to aid the Rebellion in its fight against the Galactic Empire.

6.name: Blue Harvest
6.overview: Peter retells the story of Star Wars in this hour-long episode of Family Guy. When Princess Leia is captured by the evil Darth Vader the droids C-3PO and R2-D2 escape to the planet Tatooine to find help from the Rebel Alliance. There they encounter Luke Skywalker, Han Solo, Chewbacca and Obi-Wan Kenobi who infiltrate the Death Star then rescue the captive princess from the Death Star and return to aid the Rebellion in its fight against the Galactic Empire.

6.name: Movin' Out (Brian's Song)
6.overview: Peter convinces Brian to move in with his girlfriend Jillian and Stewie tags along to help pay the rent but he tries to interfere with their relationship. Meanwhile Meg and Chris get jobs at the convenience store where Chris befriends the manager Carl who leaves Meg to do all the work. Carl soon fires her and promotes Chris to the co-boss.

6.name: Believe It or Not, Joe's Walking on Air
6.overview: When Joe feels that his disability is holding Bonnie back from living a full life, he decides to get a leg transplant. Excited about his new ability to walk, Joe engages in extreme sports, and soon ditches his current friends, for more active ones. Peter and the guys are upset at how Joe is acting and decide to teach him a lesson.

6.name: Stewie Kills Lois (1)
6.overview: Lois and Peter sail off on a cruise. Jealous that he was left behind, Stewie's matricidal tendencies are rekindled. When Brian challenges him to actually do it, Stewie carries out his diabolical plan.

6.name: Lois Kills Stewie (2)
6.overview: Presumed dead, Lois returns from the wilderness and names Stewie as her killer. On the lookout for Stewie, Joe leads a manhunt around Quahog. Desperate to stop Stewie, Lois realizes what she must do.

6.name: Padre de Familia
6.overview: Peter starts an anti-immigration group after being caught up in the pro-American sentiment at a Veterans' Day parade, but his tune quickly changes when he finds out he was born in Mexico. Unable to prove his citizenship, pass the naturalization test or convince investigators that his marriage to Lois is for real, he ends up working as a groundskeeper on his father-in-law's estate and leading the fight for immigrant rights.

6.name: Peter's Daughter
6.overview: After Quahog is hit by a flood, Meg winds up in the hospital in a coma. When she wakes up and sees a cute med student, they immediately hit it off. Overprotective of his daughter, Peter is determined to drive the med student away. That is until Meg reveals that she is pregnant.

6.name: McStroke
6.overview: Peter decides to grow a mustache, and after being mistaken for a fireman, ends up lending a hand when a fire breaks out at a local fast food restaurant. The owner gives him unlimited burgers as a thank you, but he eats too many and has a stroke. When Peter recovers, he vows to expose the fast-food company for what it really is, becoming friends with a genetically engineered cow along the way.

6.name: Back to the Woods
6.overview: James Woods returns to seek revenge against Peter. James Woods finds Peter's wallet, which he lost at Barry Manilow concert, and procedes to steal Peter's identity, and his family. Peter decides to ruin James' career and image.

6.name: Play It Again, Brian
6.overview: Peter and Lois's marriage hits a bit of a snag. When Brian wins the New England Rising Writer's award, the three of them head to Martha's Vineyard for a nice relaxing vacation. Peter's usual drinking habits take over, and he soon spirals out of control, as does Brian's self restraint, as he soon professes his love for Lois. Peter and Brian test their friendship over the woman of their dreams.

6.name: The Former Life of Brian
6.overview: Brian, afraid that he may have lost his last chance at love, searches for a former flame, Tracy. To his surprise, he discovers that he is the father to her son, Dylan. Tracy decides that Brian should raise the boy, and she drops him off at the Griffin's house. When Dylan runs rough-shot in the house, Brian's attitude towards parenting doesn't settle too well with Lois and Peter.

6.name: Long John Peter
6.overview: When Peter gets a pet parrot, he begins acting like a pirate. Peter accidentally kills the bird after raising hell through all of Quahog. Meanwhile, when Brian is visiting the vet, Chris falls in love with the intern, Anna.

7.name: Love Blactually
7.overview: Dissatisfied with his current love life, Brian decides to slow things down when he meets the girl of his dreams, Carolyn. However, Cleveland soon sweeps Carolyn off her feet, and Brian must do anything he can do, to win her back, even if it means involving Cleveland's ex-wife, Loretta.

7.name: I Dream of Jesus
7.overview: When Peter re-discovers his love for the painfully annoying yet seductively catchy tune "Surfin' Bird," Brian and Stewie decide to take matters into their own hands to ensure that the song will never be heard again! On the verge of tears, Peter goes to every record store in Quahog to track down his beloved jingle, and he runs into a familiar face and ends up hanging out with Jesus Christ.

7.name: Road to Germany
7.overview: Mort Goldman accidentally steps into Stewie's time machine and goes back in time to 1939 Poland, and Brian and Stewie follow to retrieve him. When they find him, the Nazis invade and Mort is taken to Germany, where Stewie and Brian must go to save him, as well as find a way back to the present.

7.name: Baby Not On Board
7.overview: Peter gets a lasting supply of gasoline for the year, so the family decides to use it to take a road trip to the Grand Canyon. They pack up and hit the road, but it takes them a while to realize that they had forgotten to bring Stewie with them; when they do, they must make their way home posthaste.

7.name: The Man with Two Brians
7.overview: Eight-year-old Brian (who is 56 in dog years) seems to have lost a step or two, so Peter gets a new talking dog, New Brian, to "take some of the load off" the old one, who doesn't like the idea at all.

7.name: Tales of a Third Grade Nothing
7.overview: Peter's boss Angela sends him back to the third grade so that he can qualify for a work promotion. Meanwhile, Brian, Frank Sinatra Jr. and Stewie open the coolest club in Quahog.

7.name: Ocean's Three and a Half
7.overview: After being pregnant for seven years, Bonnie finally gives birth to a beautiful baby girl named Susie!  Stewie quickly falls for the little tyke and vows to do anything to win her heart and get in her diaper. Meanwhile, Joe borrows $20,000 from a bookie to pay for Bonnie's medical bills, but then plans to steal the money from Mr. Pewterschmidt to pay his debt.

7.name: Family Gay
7.overview: After buying a brain-damaged horse, which causes over $100,000 worth of damage, Peter participates in medical experiments to make some extra cash. One of the genes he is injected with turns him gay, which later results in him leaving Lois for another man. Although the family is crushed, they quickly realise the most important thing is that Peter is happy.

7.name: The Juice Is Loose
7.overview: Peter finds an old winning raffle ticket from 1989 that entitles him a golf outing with O.J. Simpson. After befriending him, Peter brings O.J. home home to meet the family. However, the people of Quahog aren't too fond of the former running back.

7.name: FOX-y Lady
7.overview: Lois gets a job working for FOX News and has to do a report on a liberal filmmaker. However, she loses the job when her story implicates a conservative radio host.

7.name: Not All Dogs Go to Heaven
7.overview: Meg becomes extremely religious, and tries to convert Brian from Atheism. Meanwhile, after the family visits a Star Trek convention, Stewie kidnaps the entire cast of Star Trek: The Next Generation and forces them to hang out with him.
Brian and Stewie attempt to rally Quahog to try and legalize marijuana.

7.name: Stew-Roids
7.overview: Stewie starts going to the gym with Peter after he gets beaten up by Joe's baby daughter Susie, but just when it seems that his hard work is paying off, Peter reveals that he's been giving him anabolic steroid. Meanwhile, Chris begins dating a popular girl at school, only to see her popularity subside.

7.name: We Love You, Conrad
7.overview: When Brian discovers that his beloved Jillian is getting married, he hits the sauce to drown his sorrows. The next morning, he wakes up with more than a hangover. After realizing he has hooked up with "The Hills" star Lauren Conrad, he wants to run and hide. When Brian discovers Lauren is nothing like how she's portrayed on TV, he thinks he may have found his perfect match – if only he can let Jillian go.

7.name: Three Kings
7.overview: Peter imagines his friends and family in three of Stephen King's most famous works. Stand by Me parody - Twelve-year old Peter, Quagmire, Cleveland and Joe travel along a railroad track on a journey of self-discovery. Misery parody - Brian is injured in a bad car crash only to be "rescued" by his "number one fan," Stewie. The Shawshank Redemption parody - Cleveland and Peter become fast friends in prison.

7.name: Peter's Progress
7.overview: Peter learns from a psychic through a palm reading that he had a fascinating past life in the 17th century as Griffin Peterson, a dignified English gentleman who vied for the love of Lady Redbush in the newly founded American colony of Quahog.

8.name: Road to the Multiverse
8.overview: With the help of an out-of-this-world remote control, Stewie and Brian travel through alternate universes, including a post-apocalyptic world and a parallel world run by dogs where humans are pets.

8.name: Family Goy
8.overview: When Lois finds a lump on her breast, she goes to the doctor to test for cancer. The tests come back benign, but the doctor looks at her family medical history and reveals some news about Lois' heritage.

8.name: Spies Reminiscent of Us
8.overview: Chevy Chase and Dan Aykroyd move to Quahog. Brian and Stewie find out about their secret agency. They are told that there are Russian Sleeper Cells all over US. They go talk to Mayor West, and find out he is one of the sleeper cells. They follow Mayor West to Russia where they disable a nuke aimed at the US.

8.name: Brian's Got a Brand New Bag
8.overview: When Brian begins dating an older woman, the family begins to make fun of him. After Brian's new fling breaks her hip, he gets the pleasure of becoming her errand boy.

8.name: Hannah Banana
8.overview: After attending to the concert of a teen superstar, Stewie discovers a surprising secret about the singer. Meanwhile back at home, Chris tries to prove the existence of the Evil Monkey.

8.name: Quagmire's Baby
8.overview: Father doesn't know best when Quagmire assumes responsibility for a baby left at his doorstep. Meanwhile, Stewie creates a stupid clone of himself that he uses as a slave. When he ends up creating a clone for Brian too, they find out that the clones are unstable, at which point the clones break down.

8.name: Jerome Is the New Black
8.overview: Peter and the guys interview potential friends to fill the vacancy left by Cleveland. Meanwhile, Brian discovers that Quagmire hates him.

8.name: Dog Gone
8.overview: When Brian accidentally kills another dog, he is surprised to see that no one cares, so he tries to convince everyone that the life of a dog should have the same value as a human one. Meanwhile, Lois hires a maid who doesn’t listen to Peter

8.name: Business Guy
8.overview: When Peter gives Lois’ dad the bachelor party he never had, Mr. Pewterschmidt goes into a coma. Even Dr. House can't help. Lois gains control of Pewterschmidt Industries which she passes to Peter. He gets power-hungry and fires the board. Mr. Pewterschmidt bounces back and tries to regain control of what he’s built.

8.name: Big Man on Hippocampus
8.overview: When Peter hits his head and suffers amnesia after getting in a fight with Richard Dawson on Family Feud, he forgets everything about his life, including his family and friends. In an attempt to try to bring back his memory, Lois decides to reintroduce Peter to his old self, until he begins to hit on other women, much to Lois's dismay. She decides to take the kids and leave Peter, giving Quagmire the opportunity to score with Lois. Peter now must prove to Lois he can remember her and make himself worthy of her again.

8.name: Dial Meg for Murder
8.overview: Brian gets a gig writing for a teen magazine about the average American girl, but when he starts trailing Meg for research he discovers that her desperate attempts at dating have led her to a guy in the slammer. When Brian tries to expose Meg’s secret to the family, he finds out that her boyfriend, Luke, has broken out of jail and has been hiding in the Griffin’s house.

8.name: Extra Large Medium
8.overview: When Chris and Stewie go missing for several days, Lois is at her wit's end until she sees a psychic who assures her they are safe. Even after the boys return home, Lois and Peter's psychic obsession continues, and Peter takes it a step too far when he starts charging neighbors for his extrasensory perception. Meanwhile, Chris develops a crush on a girl at school.

8.name: Go, Stewie, Go!
8.overview: Stewie cross-dresses in order to star in the American version of Jolly Farm Revue. Meanwhile, Peter makes Lois feel bad about her age.

8.name: Peter-assment
8.overview: After Peter has some luck selling footage of a celebrity he caught off-guard, he decides to become a full-time paparazzo. To make matters worse, his new boss, Angela, finds him irresistible and plagues him with unwanted advances. When Peter rebuffs her, he winds up getting the ax. Meanwhile, Stewie makes his theatrical debut in a school play, only to be overcome with stage fright.

8.name: Brian Griffin's House of Payne
8.overview: Lois encourages Brian to submit his pilot script to a network. James Woods gets involved and things don't exactly go according to Brian's original plan. Meanwhile, Stewie has a bit of a mishap.

8.name: April in Quahog
8.overview: When the local news announces that the Earth will be destroyed by a black hole in 24 hours, everyone in Quahog goes nuts trying to live out their last day.

8.name: Brian & Stewie
8.overview:
In the 150th episode, Brian & Stewie get locked in a bank vault, they are forced to deal with each other on a whole new level.

Unlike any other FAMILY GUY episode, the acts will play out in real time as Brian and Stewie react to being locked in a small space with no baby food or martinis to keep them from losing their minds while they hatch an escape plan.

8.name: Quagmire's Dad
8.overview: Quagmire can accept his dad's lifestyle choices, but not when they involve Brian.

8.name: The Splendid Source
8.overview: Peter, Joe, and Quagmire search for the source of a dirty joke.

8.name: Something, Something, Something, Dark Side
8.overview: Peter makes good on another power outage at home--by entertaining his family with a retelling of Star Wars Episode V: The Empire Strikes Back. Newcomers to the story include Mort as Lando Calrissian, Chris' boss Carl as Yoda, and the Giant Chicken as the nefarious Boba Fett.

8.name: Something, Something, Something, Dark Side
8.overview: Peter makes good on another power outage at home--by entertaining his family with a retelling of Star Wars Episode V: The Empire Strikes Back. Newcomers to the story include Mort as Lando Calrissian, Chris' boss Carl as Yoda, and the Giant Chicken as the nefarious Boba Fett.

9.name: And Then There Were Fewer
9.overview: In this murder mystery special, The Griffins as well as all the other major characters become trapped in a mansion while they are killed off one by one.

9.name: And Then There Were Fewer
9.overview: In this murder mystery special, The Griffins as well as all the other major characters become trapped in a mansion while they are killed off one by one.

9.name: Excellence in Broadcasting
9.overview: Brian decides to confront Rush Limbaugh when he comes to Quahog for a book signing.

9.name: Welcome Back Carter
9.overview: After Peter catches Carter having an affair, he is forced into keeping a secret. But when Peter accidentally spills the beans, Lois' mom demands a divorce, and Peter is in charge of Carter's return to bachelorhood.

9.name: Halloween on Spooner Street
9.overview:
Peter and Joe team up to execute a series of painful and humiliating Halloween pranks on unsuspecting family, friends and neighbors.

While Brian shows Stewie the ropes of trick-or-treating, Meg sets out to attend her first high school Halloween party with high hopes for the evening.

9.name: Baby, You Knock Me Out
9.overview: After Lois dominates her competition in the boxing ring, Peter decides to become her promoter.

9.name: Brian Writes a Bestseller
9.overview: Feeling discouraged with the progress of his writing career, Brian decides to throw in the towel until Stewie become his manager and one of his books hits the bestseller list. But when the fame goes to his head, Brian gets a wake-up call from Bill Maher.

9.name: Road to the North Pole
9.overview: Stewie’s favorite thing after his teddy bear, Rupert, is Christmas. So when he hears that Santa is making an appearance at the mall, he begs Brian to take him so that he can tell Santa in person what he wants for Christmas. Brian begrudgingly takes Stewie to the mall, only to get the brush-off from Santa. As a result, Stewie and Brian travel to the North Pole to find Santa and teach him a lesson in holiday cheer.

9.name: Road to the North Pole
9.overview: Stewie’s favorite thing after his teddy bear, Rupert, is Christmas. So when he hears that Santa is making an appearance at the mall, he begs Brian to take him so that he can tell Santa in person what he wants for Christmas. Brian begrudgingly takes Stewie to the mall, only to get the brush-off from Santa. As a result, Stewie and Brian travel to the North Pole to find Santa and teach him a lesson in holiday cheer.

9.name: New Kidney in Town
9.overview: Peter needs a new kidney after his energy-drink addiction causes renal failure. Meanwhile, Chris is chosen to introduce United States President Barack Obama at his school after winning an essay contest. Trouble is, Meg wrote the essay.

9.name: And I'm Joyce Kinney
9.overview: Lois is outraged when local news anchor Joyce Kinney airs a negative profile on her.

9.name: Friends of Peter G.
9.overview: Brian and Peter are required to attend a 12-step program to deal with their drinking problems; Death gives Peter a glimpse of what life would be like without alcohol.

9.name: German Guy
9.overview: Chris's search for a new hobby leads him to befriend a puppeteer named Franz, until his checkered past is revealed.

9.name: The Hand That Rocks the Wheelchair
9.overview: Meg is enlisted to check in on Susie and Joe while Bonnie is away but she soon becomes a little too involved. Meanwhile, Stewie accidentally creates an evil twin.

9.name: Trading Places
9.overview: Chris, Meg, Peter and Lois all trade places to teach each other a lesson.

9.name: Tiegs for Two
9.overview: When the local dry cleaner loses Peter’s favorite white shirt, he goes ballistic. Meanwhile, Brian tries to score on a date with a new friend, Denise, but ends up striking out. Instead of calling it quits on love, he decides to enlist Quagmire’s help, but things get nasty when they start to compete for the affections of each other’s old flames, Cheryl Tiegs and Jillian Russell.

9.name: Brothers & Sisters
9.overview: When Mayor West marries Lois’ sister, Carol, they move into the Griffin’s house to be one big “happy” family. To make room for everyone, Stewie is bumped out of his room and takes over Chris’ room, but not everyone is annoyed by the change. Peter and Mayor West bond and become best friends.

9.name: The Big Bang Theory
9.overview: Stewie and Brian go back in time to stop Bertram from killing Leonardo da Vinci in order to erase Stewie from the universe.

9.name: Foreign Affairs
9.overview: Bonnie and Lois take a little trip to Paris where Bonnie crosses the line.

9.name: It's a Trap!
9.overview: Peter retells the story of Return of the Jedi.

9.name: It's a Trap!
9.overview: Peter retells the story of Return of the Jedi.

1.name: Space Pilot 3000
1.overview: On New Year's Eve 1999, Pizza Delivery boy Philip J. Fry accidentally falls into a cryogenic chamber and is frozen for 1,000 years. Finally unfrozen, he explores New New York, meets his new best friend (a kleptomaniacal robot named Bender) and goes to work for his great-great-great-great-grand-nephew's space delivery business.

1.name: The Series Has Landed
1.overview: For the crew's first mission, they must deliver a crate full of toys for an arcade game to the moon. Fry is very excited, while it is not really a big deal in the year 3000. Fry tries to persuade Leela to go exploring on the moon, while Amy tries to recover the keys to the Planet Express Ship.

1.name: I, Roommate
1.overview: When it is discovered that Philip has been living in the Planet Express office, he is forced to move out. Trying to sleep standing up in Bender's closet-sized apartment doesn't work, so they go looking for a place both can be comfortable. Unfortunately, Bender is incompatible with the ideal apartment they do find until he has a radical... modification which makes him less than happy.

1.name: Love's Labours Lost in Space
1.overview: On a mission intended to save endangered animals on a collapsing planet, Leela and the crew run into legendary starship captain Zapp Brannigan. A self-proclaimed ladies man, Zapp sees Leela as a potential new conquest. When the captain refuses to aid the animal rescue, Leela and her crew try to leave Zapp's starship. But Zapp throws Fry and Bender in jail, and summons Leela to his "Lovenasium". They ultimately escape and arrive on the doomed planet, where Leela finally finds love - with a cute, and very useful, creature named Nibbler.

1.name: Fear of a Bot Planet
1.overview: Fry and Leela disguise themselves as robots to find Bender after he fails to return from a delivery to a robot planet, only to learn that he has become a celebrity in a culture which fears humans.

1.name: A Fishful of Dollars
1.overview: Fry discovers his bank account interest has made him a billionaire and buys the one thing the year 3000 doesn't have, anchovies.

1.name: My Three Suns
1.overview: Delivery Boy Philip J. Fry is promoted to Emperor after accidentally drinking his fluid-based predecessor.

1.name: A Big Piece of Garbage
1.overview: A big piece of garbage that was released a thousand years ago is now on the way to Earth to destroy it. After Fry, Leela and Bender fail on placing a bomb on it, the city has to build another big piece of garbage, yet that may be a problem since trash doesn't exist in year 3000.

1.name: Hell Is Other Robots
1.overview: During a Beastie Boys concert, Bender runs into a high school buddy, Fender. He invites him to a party, which turns out to be robots getting high off of electricity, or "jacking on". After one try, Bender is hooked.

10.name: Destroy Tall Monsters
10.overview: Bender undergoes a risky treatment to make himself gigantic.

10.name: The World Is Hot Enough
10.overview: The crew ignites a volcano in a desperate attempt to slow global warming.

10.name: Fifty Shades of Green
10.overview: Fry is heartbroken to learn Leela's soulmate is someone other than him.

10.name: The Numberland Gap
10.overview: The crew journeys into a mysterious world inhabited by numbers.

10.name: Scared Screenless
10.overview: Bender is sent off to Zapp Brannigan's camp for screen addicts.

10.name: Wicked Human
10.overview: Pandemonium erupts when people around the world start floating up into the sky.

10.name: Murderoni
10.overview: A conspiracy theory makes shocking claims about the basement of the local pizzeria.

10.name: Crab Splatter
10.overview: Leela's parents adopt Dr. Zoidberg.

10.name: The Trouble with Truffles
10.overview: Bender and his trusty pig set off to hunt space truffles in the truffle belt.

10.name: The White Hole
10.overview: The crew embarks on an epic ten-million-year journey to witness the birth of a new universe.

2.name: A Flight to Remember
2.overview: The Planet Express crew take a space cruise vacation. Leela tells Captain Zapp Brannigan that Fry is her boyfriend and Amy tells the same thing to her parents. Bender falls in love with a rich female robot. Problems start when the spaceship approaches a black hole.

2.name: Mars University
2.overview: Fry, Leela, Bender and Farnsworth have to go to Mars University where Fry has to share a room with a talking monkey, Farnsworth's last invention. The monkey becomes a real headache when he starts beating Fry in all areas. Meanwhile, Bender is hanging out with nerd robots.

2.name: When Aliens Attack
2.overview: The Omicronians invade Earth, demanding to see the lost final episode of the 1999 TV show Single Female Lawyer. The Planet Express crew must re-enact the episode to appease the invading aliens.

2.name: Fry & the Slurm Factory
2.overview: When Fry wins a free tour of the Slurm soda factory, he and his friends split off from the tour group and make a horrifying discovery concerning the "secret ingredient" that makes Slurm so addictive.

2.name: I Second That Emotion
2.overview: Professor Farnsworth installs an empathy chip in Bender after the inconsiderate robot flushes Nibbler down the toilet. With his newfound emotions, a concerned Bender ventures into the mutant-infested sewers to rescue Leela's beloved pet.

2.name: Brannigan, Begin Again
2.overview: Upon destroying the new DOOP headquarters, Zapp Brannigan and Kif find themselves court-martialed and dishonorably discharged. They take up jobs at Planet Express, where Kif learns what respect feels like and Zapp incites a mutiny against Leela.

2.name: A Head in the Polls
2.overview: When the price of titanium skyrockets, Bender pawns his titanium-rich body and lives the high life as just a head. But when the head-in-a-jar of Richard M. Nixon uses Bender's body to run for election for President of Earth, Bender vows to get his rightful property back.

2.name: Xmas Story
2.overview: To apologize for an inconsiderate remark, Fry searches for the perfect Xmas gift for Leela, all the while trying to avoid the murderous SantaBot, whose standards for niceness are astronomically high.

2.name: Why Must I Be a Crustacean in Love?
2.overview: When Dr. Zoidberg runs amok during the Decapodian mating season, Fry sets out to teach him how to woo a mate. Things backfire when Zoidberg's desired mate falls for Fry instead, and Zoidberg challenges Fry to a battle to the death.

2.name: Put Your Head on My Shoulders
2.overview: Fry's brief relationship with Amy is forcibly prolonged when a near-fatal car crash leaves Fry's head grafted to Amy's left shoulder. Meanwhile, Bender cashes in on Valentine's Day by starting a dating service.

2.name: Lesser of Two Evils
2.overview: Fry suspects Bender's new buddy, a bending unit named Flexo who looks almost exactly like Bender, of being pure evil. When a valuable atom of Jumbonium goes missing, Fry suspects Flexo, but can't tell which robot is which.

2.name: Raging Bender
2.overview: Bender is the featured attraction in the Ultimate Robot Fighting League, but his glory is short-lived when he is instructed to intentionally lose his next match. Leela vows to lead Bender to victory, in an effort to show up her unsupportive martial arts teacher.

2.name: A Bicyclops Built For Two
2.overview: Leela meets Alkazar, who claims to be the only other remaining cyclops in the universe. But when Alkazar invites Leela back to his home planet and treats her like a slave, Fry suspects that something is amiss.

2.name: How Hermes Requisitioned His Groove Back
2.overview: When Hermes takes a stress-relieving vacation, replacement bureaucrat Morgan Proctor becomes infatuated with Fry. Bender threatens to publicize their affair, but Morgan removes Bender's memory and hides it within the cavernous Central Bureaucracy.

2.name: A Clone of My Own
2.overview: Professor Farnsworth unveils his clone, Cubert Farnsworth, whom he plans to leave everything to upon his retirement. But Cubert wants no part of Farnsworth's lifestyle, prompting the professor into a premature retirement on the Near-Death Star.

2.name: The Deep South
2.overview: A fishing trip over the ocean takes a turn for the worse when a colossal mouth bass pulls the Planet Express ship to the bottom of the sea. There, Fry falls in love with a mermaid named Umbriel, and the crew discovers the sunken city of Atlanta, Georgia.

2.name: Bender Gets Made
2.overview: Bender takes a new job in the Robot Mafia, but his loyalty is tested when he goes along on the robotic gangster's efforts to rob the Planet Express ship.

2.name: The Problem with Popplers
2.overview: The crew discovers an irresistible source of food on a distant planet, and brings it back to Earth to be sold at the Fishy Joe's restaurant chain. But when it's discovered that the so-called "Popplers" are actually Omicronian babies, the Omicronians demand recompense.

2.name: Mother's Day
2.overview: Mom reprograms the world's robots to rebel against humanity. The only hope of salvation is Mom's old flame - Professor Farnsworth, who must rekindle his romance with Mom in order to save mankind.

2.name: Anthology of Interest I
2.overview: The Professor's "What-If" machine simulates Bender, Leela and Fry's wishes. Bender discovers what it would be like if he were 500 feet tall, Leela discovers what it would be like if she were more impulsive, and Fry discovers what would happen if he never came to the future.

3.name: The Honking
3.overview: When the inheritance Bender claims in the Old Country isn't quite what he expected, the Planet Express crew has to find the source of an age-old curse.

3.name: War Is the H-Word
3.overview: Fry and Bender enlist in the army to get a soldiers discount for bubblegum. When they are called to fight a war on a planet of bouncing ball aliens, Leela disguises herself as man to enlist with them. Bender is eventually sent to parley with the enemy, but this turns out to be a ploy.

3.name: The Cryonic Woman
3.overview: Fry is reunited with his old girlfriend from the 20th Century, but she doesn't adapt to the 31st century as well as he has. Convincing Fry to return to the cryogenic chamber, they awaken in a barren wasteland and struggle to make a place for themselves.

3.name: Parasites Lost
3.overview: When Fry becomes infested with parasitic worms that make him stronger and smarter, he finally finds the perfect way to profess his feelings to Leela. Meanwhile, the rest of the crew goes on a journey into Fry's body to eradicate the worms.

3.name: Amazon Women in the Mood
3.overview: A double-date for Kif, Amy, Zapp, and Leela ends in disaster when their orbiting restaurant crashes on planet Amazonia. The hulking female inhabitants of the planet take their male captives to the omniscient Femputer, who orders Fry, Zapp, and Kif to death by "snu-snu".

3.name: Bendless Love
3.overview: Bender's urge to bend prompts Professor Farnsworth to send him on a rehabilitation visit to a steel factory, where he falls in love with a shapely fem-bot named Angleyne. But Bender's old rival Flexo and the intrusion of the Robot Mafia threaten to throw a wrench into the proceedings.

3.name: The Day the Earth Stood Stupid
3.overview: Earth is invaded by super-intelligent flying brains, who sap the Earth's populace of their intelligence. Leela is taken to Nibbler's home planet Eternia, where the Nibblonians explain that only one human is immune to the brains' powers - Fry.

3.name: That's Lobstertainment!
3.overview: Dr. Zoidberg reunites with his uncle, silent hologram star Harold Zoid, and the two of them set out to make a movie together. They cast the temperamental Calculon in the lead role, who demands an Oscar for his performance, but the movie doesn't go over well with audiences.

3.name: The Birdbot of Ice-Catraz
3.overview: A sober Bender crashes a dark matter tanker on Pluto, threatening the penguin reserve nearby. Leela helps out in the clean-up, but when the penguins begin mating out of control, drastic action must be taken to thin the herd.

3.name: The Luck of the Fryrish
3.overview: After a string of bad luck, Fry ventures into the decaying ruins of Old New York to regain his lucky seven-leaf clover from his childhood, only to find that his brother Yancy Fry had stolen not only the clover, but Fry's identity as well. Fry sets out to exhume his brother's body, but discovers the startling truth about Yancy instead.

3.name: The Cyber House Rules
3.overview: Leela meets up with her former orphanarium playmate Adlai Atkins, now a plastic surgeon, who offers to grant Leela surgery that will give her two eyes. Meanwhile, Bender adopts twelve orphans in order to collect $1200 in government stipends.

3.name: Insane in the Mainframe
3.overview: Accused of robbing a bank, Fry and Bender plead insanity and are both sent to a robot insane asylum. While Bender and his buddy Roberto plan an escape, Fry is brainwashed into thinking that he is a robot.

3.name: Bendin' in the Wind
3.overview: When Bender is paralyzed in a tragic can opener accident, he discovers his musical washboard skills and goes on tour as a member of Beck's folk-rock band, acting as a voice for broken robots everywhere. Fry, Leela, Amy, and Zoidberg tag along in Fry's antique 1960s VW Van.

3.name: Time Keeps On Slippin'
3.overview: While creating a team of mutants to play the Harlem Globetrotters, the Professor accidentally causes a disruption in time that threatens the existence of the universe. Meanwhile, Fry tries to win an unreceptive Leela's heart.

3.name: I Dated a Robot
3.overview: Fry discovers the ability to download any celebrity onto a blank robot, and chooses to download Lucy Liu, with whom he falls madly in love. Repulsed by this disgusting display of human/robot love, Leela, Bender, and Zoidberg set out to shut down Nappster.com and put an end to illegal celebrity downloads forever.

4.name: Roswell That Ends Well
4.overview: A spacetime accident leaves the Planet Express crew stranded in Roswell, New Mexico in the year 1947, where a shattered Bender is mistaken for UFO debris and Zoidberg is captured as an alien specimen. Leela and the professor work to get everybody home, while Fry tries to keep his accident-prone grandfather alive.

4.name: A Tale of Two Santas
4.overview: A Planet Express mission to Robot Santa's colony on Neptune leaves the murderous robot trapped in the frozen sea, and Bender takes over as Santa, vowing to bring peace and goodwill to Xmas again. But when Bender is mistaken for the real Robot Santa, he is arrested and sentenced to death.

4.name: Love and Rocket
4.overview: As Valentine's Day approaches, Bender's romance with the Planet Express ship puts the crew in jeopardy.

4.name: Leela's Homeworld
4.overview: Leela finally learns the truth about her parents and her own identity.

4.name: Where the Buggalo Roam
4.overview: When Amy's parents' ranch is hit by a dust storm that blows away their herd of buggalo, Kif sets out to prove his masculinity by rounding up the herd, only to become entangled with the native Martians.

4.name: A Pharaoh to Remember
4.overview: Bender fears that nobody will remember him after he dies, and sees his chance for immortality when the crew is enslaved on the planet Osiris 4. Posing as the planet's new pharaoh, Bender orders a humongous statue built in his honor, and quickly goes mad with power.

4.name: Godfellas
4.overview: Bender is accidentally shot out of the ship's torpedo tube and becomes lost in space. Floating through the ethereal darkness, Bender becomes inhabited with tiny alien life forms, but has trouble playing God to their unyielding prayers.

4.name: Future Stock
4.overview: With Planet Express in financial trouble, Fry nominates a flashy businessman from the 1980s to replace Professor Farnsworth as CEO of the company.

4.name: A Leela of Her Own
4.overview: Leela endeavors to become the first female blernsball player, but her lack of depth perception hinders her skills. Nevertheless, she becomes the pitcher for the New New York Mets, purely for her novelty value.

4.name: 30% Iron Chef
4.overview: Bender, insulted by the reception his terrible cooking receives, runs away and joins a space hobo camp, where he apprentices with a legendary former TV chef. Meanwhile, Dr. Zoidberg accidentally destroys Professor Farnsworth's prized ship-in-a-bottle and frames Fry.

4.name: Where No Fan Has Gone Before
4.overview: The crew is pitted against the original Star Trek cast to win the devotion of an energy creature.

4.name: Anthology of Interest II
4.overview: When the Professor fine tunes his "What-If" machine, Bender discovers what it would be like if he were human, Fry discovers what it would be like if life were more like a video game, and Leela discovers what it would be like if she found her true home.

5.name: Crimes of the Hot
5.overview: When Earth is jeopardized by global warming, the crew attends a conference in Kyoto hosted by Al Gore's head.

5.name: Jurassic Bark
5.overview: Bender grows jealous when Fry attempts to clone Seymour, his beloved fossilized dog.

5.name: The Route of All Evil
5.overview: Farnsworth's clone Cubert teams up with Hermes' son Dwight to launch a newspaper delivery business. Farnsworth and Hermes scoff at the kids' efforts - until the delivery boys accumulate enough capital to buy out Planet Express. Meanwhile, Fry and Leela use Bender to brew their own homemade beer.

5.name: A Taste of Freedom
5.overview: Zoidberg becomes the center of a Supreme Court case after he celebrates Freedom Day by publicly eating the Earth flag.

5.name: Kif Gets Knocked Up a Notch
5.overview: When Kif gets pregnant, Amy questions her readiness to become a parent.

5.name: Less Than Hero
5.overview: When Fry and Leela discover a cream that gives them unusual powers, they form a superhero team along with Bender.

5.name: Teenage Mutant Leela's Hurdles
5.overview: The Planet Express crew is exposed to a substance that causes everyone to age backwards.

5.name: The Why of Fry
5.overview: Nibbler enlists Fry in a mission to blow up evil brains bent on destroying the universe.

5.name: The Sting
5.overview: The Planet Express crew attempt the mission that killed the last crew: raiding a massive alien beehive for its honey.

5.name: The Farnsworth Parabox
5.overview: The Planet Express crew goes to a parallel universe made accessible by the Professor's latest experiment.

5.name: Three Hundred Big Boys
5.overview: With the help of a $300 government refund, the Planet Express crew pursue their dreams and desires.

5.name: Spanish Fry
5.overview: Fry's nose is stolen by alien poachers to sell as a black market aphrodesiac.

5.name: Bend Her
5.overview: Bender's scam to win Olympic gold sets him on the road to becoming a celebrity wife.

5.name: Obsoletely Fabulous
5.overview: A new model of robot threatens Bender, forcing him to run away after he avoids upgrading his own technology.

5.name: Bender Should Not Be Allowed on TV
5.overview: Bender becomes a television star as well as a terrible role model for children.

5.name: The Devil's Hands Are Idle Playthings
5.overview: Fry makes a deal with the Robot Devil so he can become a skillful musician and win Leela's heart.

6.name: Rebirth
6.overview: After a devastating spaceship crash, Professor Farnsworth attempts to resuscitate the crew with his birth machine.

6.name: In-A-Gadda-Da-Leela
6.overview: Leela and Zapp Brannigan find themselves stranded on an Eden-like planet.

6.name: Attack of the Killer App
6.overview: The latest trend in social networking and having no life takes over Earth; and Fry posts an embarrassing video of Leela on-line.

6.name: Proposition Infinity
6.overview: Bender leads a campaign to make robosexual marriage between humans and robots legal on Earth.

6.name: The Duh-Vinci Code
6.overview: The Planet Express crew races to future Rome to unearth the shocking secret of Leonardo da Vinci.

6.name: Lethal Inspection
6.overview: Bender learns that he suffers from a mortal manufacturing defect.

6.name: The Late Philip J. Fry
6.overview: The Professor invents a one-way time machine that takes the crew ever further into the future with no hope of returning.

6.name: That Darn Katz!
6.overview: Earth is invaded by a race of intelligent cats.

6.name: A Clockwork Origin
6.overview: The theory of evolution is put to the test on a planet inhabited by robots.

6.name: The Prisoner of Benda
6.overview: A revolutionary invention allows the crew members to exchange minds.

6.name: Lrrreconcilable Ndndifferences
6.overview: After a bungled Earth invasion, alien leader Lrrr faces a midlife crisis.

6.name: The Mutants Are Revolting
6.overview: Leela leads an army of underground mutants in a rebellion against the surface people.

6.name: The Futurama Holiday Spectacular
6.overview: The Planet Express crew learns the true meaning of Xmas, Robanukah and Kwanzaa.

6.name: Neutopia
6.overview: The crew members encounter a bizarre alien with the power to change their sexual characteristics.

6.name: Benderama
6.overview: Bender creates duplicates of himself, who in turn create duplicates of themselves, until they threaten to consume all of the matter on Earth.

6.name: Ghost in the Machines
6.overview: Fed up with the crew, Bender decides to kill himself using a suicide booth. After he dies, his software takes on a ghostly existence.

7.name: The Bots and the Bees
7.overview: Bender fathers a child with the office soda machine.

7.name: A Farewell to Arms
7.overview: An ancient prophecy predicts the world will end in the year 3012.

7.name: Decision 3012
7.overview: Leela becomes campaign manager for a presidential candidate whose birth certificate is mysteriously missing.

7.name: The Thief of Baghead
7.overview: Bender joins the paparazzi and attempts to photograph a famous actor whose face has never been seen.

7.name: Zapp Dingbat
7.overview: Zapp Brannigan loses interest in Leela and instead turns his attentions towards her mother, Turanga Munda.

7.name: The Butterjunk Effect
7.overview: Leela and Amy become addicted to the performance enhancing drug known as "Nectar" to increase their chances of winning at the epic sport of Butterfly Derby.

7.name: The Six Million Dollar Mon
7.overview: Hermes replaces parts of his body with robotic counterparts so as to increase his productivity.

7.name: Fun on a Bun
7.overview: Fry is the victim of a sausage-making accident during the crew's visit to Oktoberfest.

7.name: Free Will Hunting
7.overview: Bender searches for the meaning of life after learning that robots lack free will.

7.name: Near-Death Wish
7.overview: The Professor is deeply disturbed when his parents return from virtual retirement aboard the Near-Death Star.

7.name: Viva Mars Vegas
7.overview: The crew stages a casino heist to recover stolen property from the Robot Mafia.

7.name: 31st Century Fox
7.overview: After saving a robotic fox from hunters, Bender becomes the hunted.

7.name: Naturama
7.overview: Bender and the crew are reimagined as wild animals in a nature documentary.

8.name: The Impossible Stream
8.overview: Fry risks permanent insanity when he attempts to binge-watch every TV show ever made.

8.name: Children of a Lesser Bog
8.overview: Amy and Kif’s children emerge from an alien swamp.

8.name: How the West Was 1010001
8.overview: Bender and the crew head west to join the bitcoin mining rush.

8.name: Parasites Regained
8.overview: After Nibbler falls ill, the crew shrinks down for a dangerous mission into a desert world contained within his litter box.

8.name: Related to Items You've Viewed
8.overview: Bender uncovers the mysteries of the vast Momazon corporation.

8.name: I Know What You Did Next Xmas
8.overview: Bender and Zoidberg travel through time to attack Robot Santa.

8.name: Rage Against the Vaccine
8.overview: A pandemic ravages future Earth.

8.name: Zapp Gets Canceled
8.overview: When Zapp Brannigan is canceled for crude behavior, Leela takes over as captain of the Nimbus on a critical mission.

8.name: The Prince and the Product
8.overview: The crew members are reborn as toys.

8.name: All the Way Down
8.overview: The crew investigates whether the universe is a simulation.

9.name: The One Amigo
9.overview: After Bender sells an NFT representing the "Concept of Bender", he feels lost and returns to his ancestral village in Mexico to rediscover himself.

9.name: Quids Game
9.overview: Bizarre aliens force the crew to reenact all the classic children's games Fry played at his 8th birthday party... except this time, they are played TO THE DEATH!

9.name: The Temp
9.overview: A mysterious temp worker takes over Fry's job... as well as his entire life.

9.name: Beauty and the Bug
9.overview: Bender becomes a matador in the sport of "Bug Fighting."

9.name: One Is Silicon and the Other Gold
9.overview: Leela becomes friends with a jealous chatbot.

9.name: Attack of the Clothes
9.overview: The Professor's fast-fashion clothes are a smash hit, but an environmental disaster.

9.name: Planet Espresso
9.overview: Hermes inherits a Jamaican coffee farm that holds the ruins of an ancient spaceship.

9.name: Cuteness Overload
9.overview: Amy sets out to collect the cutest toys in the universe – but they are not as harmless as they appear.

9.name: The Futurama Mystery Liberry
9.overview: Futurama is reimagined in the form of classic children's mystery books.

9.name: Otherwise
9.overview: When Fry proposes to Leela, he is overcome by a near-fatal attack of déjà vu.
